title: Preview / advanced-preview build rows for the fan-control and TRIAC targets (manual-preview / advanced-manual-preview lanes)
description: Concrete, CI-consumable build-row ledger for the four buildable fan-control and TRIAC PREVIEW / advanced-preview release targets that are delivered on the NON-WebFlash preview lanes. This is the manual-preview / advanced-manual-preview analog of config/webflash-builds.json: it records, per target, the config string, product YAML, delivery lane, preview channel, expected preview artifact name, the policy warning copy + key, the release-note draft path, the stable blocker, the build blocker (only FanTRIAC carries one, under HW-005), the firmware-build compile evidence (FanRelay/FanPWM/FanDAC; run 26821900127) or build-blocked status (FanTRIAC), and the commercial posture (hidden / not buyable / not recommended / not customer-default / not stable). It cross-references config/preview-release-targets.json (per target_id) and config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json (per candidate id). This file is build-row METADATA only: it publishes no firmware, adds no config/webflash-builds.json row, flips no config/product-catalog.json status, promotes nothing to stable, claims no bench / compliance / hardware proof (and claims no compile proof for FanTRIAC, which is build-blocked), and does not touch the WebFlash repo. config/webflash-builds.json remains the sole WebFlash release-eligibility source of truth. Read by tests/test_preview_fan_triac_build_rows.py and scripts/validate_preview_fan_triac_build_rows.py.

rows.release_note_warning: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
rows.stable_blocker: Mains-safety / installation-approval / creepage / clearance evidence + competent-person sign-off + GPIO3 strap-pin boot characterisation.
rows.notes: Relay-switched (on/off) bathroom fan via the on-board K1 relay. Buildable PREVIEW release target delivered on the manual-preview lane (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json candidate 'fanrelay'); WebFlash import stays gated by the fan-token guardrail (catalog webflash_build_matrix stays false). Firmware-build proof only (run 26821900127). Not stable, not recommended, not a customer default, not buyable; mains switching is a competent-person install.

rows.release_note_warning: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
rows.stable_blocker: Measured current / thermal evidence (S360-311-CURRENT-THERMAL-001). RPM / TachIO is not claimed (rpm_supported: false).
rows.notes: Native ESP32-S3 GPIO 12V PWM fan control (four channels). Buildable PREVIEW release target delivered on the manual-preview lane (candidate 'fanpwm'); WebFlash import stays gated by the fan-token guardrail. RPM not claimed (rpm_supported: false). Firmware-build proof only (run 26821900127). Not stable, not recommended, not a customer default, not buyable.

rows.release_note_warning: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
rows.stable_blocker: Cloudlift S12 / J3 harness + product-bench evidence; S360-312 schematic / BOM.
rows.notes: 0-10V analog (DAC) fan control for advanced duct fans (e.g. Cloudlift S12). Buildable PREVIEW release target delivered on the manual-preview lane (candidate 'fandac'); WebFlash import stays gated by the fan-token guardrail. Firmware-build proof only (run 26821900127). Not Cloudlift-ready; not stable, not recommended, not a customer default, not buyable.

rows.release_note_warning: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.
rows.stable_blocker: HW-005 (S360-320 schematic; GPIO5/GPIO6 collision; ac_dimmer cannot run across SX1509) + PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + COMPLIANCE-001 mains-voltage review.
rows.build_blocker: HW-005 BUILDABILITY blocker (NOT a stable-evidence gate and NOT a preview-policy block): S360-320 schematic uncommitted; placeholder GPIO5/GPIO6 collide with RoomIQ J10 nets; ESPHome ac_dimmer cannot run across the SX1509 expander. The target is therefore not yet buildable end-to-end, so no advanced-preview artifact can be cut on the advanced-manual-preview lane until HW-005 resolves. Stable is additionally gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + COMPLIANCE-001 mains-voltage review.
rows.notes: Mains-voltage TRIAC phase-cut dimming. ADVANCED-PREVIEW only, on the advanced-manual-preview lane, behind an explicit advanced acknowledgement gate. BUILD-BLOCKED by HW-005 (S360-320 schematic uncommitted; GPIO5/GPIO6 collide with RoomIQ J10 nets; ac_dimmer cannot run across the SX1509 expander), so no compile/firmware artifact exists and none is cut here. Not forced into the normal WebFlash preview path. Never stable, recommended, default, required-config, buyable, or WebFlash-importable. No safety/compliance proof is claimed.
